jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Analytics & Transformation
jobs.description:
**Senior Analyst, Analytics & Transformation**

**About Pret A Manger: Building the US Future of a Beloved Global Brand**

Pret US is at a defining moment. Backed by substantial investment and strategic commitment, we are not simply operating; we are transforming a beloved brand in the American market. With a newly established US Board and an entirely rebuilt senior leadership team, we are assembling exceptional talent to drive this ambitious vision forward.

This is an opportunity to join a winning team built on an ownership mindset, rapid learning, bias for action, and competitive drive. Our ideal candidates are resilient, flexible, comfortable with ambiguity, and data-driven. We are seeking leaders who thrive in dynamic, high-stakes environments and are energized by the challenge of building something remarkable.

As part of the Pret US team, you will work directly with seasoned leaders who understand turnarounds, scaling operations, and delivering results. If you are driven to exceed ambitious goals, make bold decisions, and build a brand that matters, this is where you belong.

Join us in writing the next chapter of Pret in America.

**Key Responsibilities**

Strategic Analytics & Insights

* Perform routine and ad-hoc analysis to drive business performance, including like-for-like sales and transactions growth, product mix shifts, market test performance, guest satisfaction, and guest satisfaction metrics
* Conduct studies on menu performance, pricing strategy, daypart optimization, delivery channel performance, and market expansion opportunities
* Partner with Marketing to analyze subscription program performance, digital ordering trends, loyalty initiatives, and promotional efficacy
* Provide data-driven recommendations to the North America Leadership team on ways to strengthen the Pret US business

Performance Management & Reporting

* Create and maintain dashboards and scorecards that provide real-time visibility into KPIs across marketing, operations, and performance management
* Conduct post-implementation reviews to capture learnings and drive continuous improvement across the organization
* Utilize competitor data and industry trends to provide insights on Pret's relative performance and areas of opportunity
* Proactively identify and escalate risks and potential detractors from business performance

Stakeholder Management & Communication

* Influence and align stakeholders at all levels, from Shop General Managers to members of the North America Leadership Team
* Synthesize complex analyses into succinct and specific insights and recommendations
* Clearly and effectively communicate insights and takeaways via PowerPoint and present findings with authority
* Champion a culture of data-driven decision-making, continuous improvement, and Pret's core values

**Qualifications**

Required Experience

* 2+ years of progressive experience in analytics-based roles in marketing, operations, finance, or consulting experience
* Deep expertise in business analytics, financial modeling, and data visualization tools (including but not limited to Power BI, SQL, Excel)
* Experience managing concurrent strategic priorities in fast-paced, operational environments with both retail and digital/delivery components
* Strong understanding of QSR/fast casual key performance indicators, unit economics, and omnichannel operations
* Experience in quick service restaurant, food retail, hospitality, or consumer goods industries preferred
* Experience working in organizations with strong brand values and mission-driven cultures preferred

Required Skills & Competencies

* Strategic Thinking: Ability to connect analytics insights to business strategy make clear and logical recommendations to senior leaders
* Analytical Rigor: Advanced quantitative and qualitative analysis skills with exceptional attention to detail and accuracy
* Communication Excellence: Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with ability to synthesize complex data into compelling executive narratives for diverse audiences
* Continuous Improvement: Exhibits curiosity and poses new questions and ideas for analyses independently
* Adaptability & Multitasking: Exceptional ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously while maintaining quality; comfort with ambiguity and ability to pivot quickly
* Results Orientation: Relentless focus on execution, accountability, and delivering measurable business outcomes
* Cultural Fit: Passion for Pret's mission, values, and commitment to doing the right thing

**Education**

* Bachelor's degree required

**Salary Range**

* The pay range for this role is $90,000 - $110,000. Compensation packages are based on several factors that are unique to each candidate, including but not limited to skill set, depth of experience, certifications, and the specific work location of New York, New York, United States. In addition, you will be eligible for a discretionary bonus if you are an active employee as of fiscal year-end.

jobs.title: Private Equity Analyst / Associate - NYC office
jobs.description:
**Job position offered for Private Equity Analyst / Associate - Fund raising advisory – NYC office**

**Independent placement advisory firm dedicated to assisting leading mid-market**
private equity fund managers. As a placement agent, our role is to support our clients throughout the fundraising process, from the definition of the fundraising strategy and its objectives to the final closing of the fund.

Qualifications & skills

Candidates should have a 1.5-3 years of prior full-time experience within private equity, investment banking, top tier consultancy, as well as have a strong academic background (top tier university or business school), in addition to:

* Having strong analytical and numerical skills with high attention to detail
* Being results-driven and able to perform under pressure for top-tier, high-profile private equity clients
* Having excellent Excel skills and PowerPoint skills
* Having a high level of written and verbal communication
* Being fluent in English (additional european language would be a plus)

Key features of the role

Truly international work- and team environment, focusing both on Europe and US

Exceptional learning opportunity to discover key private equity markets and strategies

US citizenship is required.

jobs.title: Credit Risk Analyst
jobs.description:
**What We're Looking For**

* Support the accurate and timely reporting and analysis of credit risk across multiple front office systems to meet enterprise risk management, senior management, and regulatory requirements.
* Identify, measure, analyze, and aggregate various counterparty credit risk measures, including margin exposure and financial metrics, as defined in Stifel's counterparty exposure framework.
* Utilize Credit Application to map transactions from various internal front office systems and files to aggregate exposures by credit group, counterparty, products, and sectors across multiple capital market products.
* Perform data validation checks, enrich existing counterparty reporting, propose new counterparty credit exposure reports, and recommend improvements to existing processes.
* Enhance and maintain documentation of exposure framework, along with ongoing validation procedures to meet Stifel requirements.
* Participate in industry reviews to assign internal risk ratings.

**What You'll Bring**

* Detail-oriented, strong analytical skills, ability to use sound logic and reasoning to identify solutions and reach conclusions.
* Strong interpersonal skills facilitating collaboration within CRM and the broader Enterprise Risk management team and across the organization (front office business, IT, operations, and management).
* Identify complex problems and critically review related information to develop and evaluate options and recommend solutions.
* Ability to interact positively and communicate with individuals at all levels of the organization and various business units, with strong attention to detail.
* Sound written and verbal communication skills.
* Able to work independently with minimal supervision, focus on accuracy, and complete assigned responsibilities within agreed time frames.

**Education & Experience**

* Minimum Required: Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Information Technology, or other quantitative discipline.
* Minimum Required: 2-4 years of work experience in banking or finance, preferably working in a capital markets environment or with capital markets products.

**Systems & Technology**

* Strong in Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook.
* Basic knowledge of SQL or similar language is a plus.

**Compensation Range**
Salary: USD $84,000.00/Yr. - USD $126,000.00/Yr.
  
  
Actual salaries may vary, and may be based on several factors, including but not limited to each candidate’s qualifications, skills, and overall competencies for the position. The base salary is one component of Stifel’s overall compensation package for each individual employee. Other benefits and offerings include, but not limited to, discretionary bonuses, health / dental / vision / prescription insurance offerings, Stifel Total Health Connect, flexible spending accounts, tuition & certification assistance programs, paid time off, and much, much more! To view a more comprehensive list of Stifel’s current offerings, please visit https://www.stifel.com/careers/benefits. Applications are accepted until the position is filled.
  
  
**About Stifel**
Stifel is more than 130 years old and still thinking like a start-up. We are a global wealth management and investment banking firm serious about innovation and fresh ideas. Built on a simple premise of safeguarding our clients’ money as if it were our own, coined by our namesake, Herman Stifel, our success is intimately tied to our commitment to helping families, companies, and municipalities find their own success.
  
  
While our headquarters is in St. Louis, we have offices in New York, San Francisco, Baltimore, London, Frankfurt, Toronto, and more than 400 other locations. Stifel is home to approximately 9,000 individuals who are currently building their careers as financial advisors, research analysts, project managers, marketing specialists, developers, bankers, operations associates, among hundreds more. Let’s talk about how you can find your place here at Stifel, where success meets success.
  
  
At Stifel we offer an entrepreneurial environment, comprehensive benefits package to include health, dental and vision care, 401k, wellness initiatives, life insurance, and paid time off.
  
  
Stifel is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

jobs.title: Asset & Wealth Management, Fixed Income, Junior Trader and Portfolio Manager, Analyst– New York
jobs.description:
**Job Description**
Asset & Wealth Management: Goldman Sachs Asset Management (GSAM) is one of the world’s leading investment managers. GSAM provides institutional and individual investors with investment and advisory solutions, with strategies spanning asset classes, industries, and geographies. We help our clients navigate today’s dynamic markets, and identify the opportunities that shape their portfolios and long-term investment goals. We extend these global capabilities to the world’s leading pension plans, sovereign wealth funds, central banks, insurance companies, financial institutions, endowments, foundations, individuals and family offices.
  
  
**Responsibilities And Qualifications**
The Global Fixed Income and Currency team is seeking a junior trader/portfolio manager at the analyst/associate level in New York, NY. The successful candidate will be an integral part of the trading/portfolio management team in New York. In addition to its trading responsibilities, the role will also include support for the portfolio management efforts and considerable interaction with operations and technology support.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**

* Allocate, execute, and reconcile trades
* Working with portfolio managers to build and monitor risk in portfolios, including management of cash flows
* Maintain and rebalance portfolios across strategies
* Work with technology and strat teams to increase automation, improve quality of data, and create new trading tools
* Liase with operations on a daily basis to resolve any trading issues
* Update and maintain reports for weekly/bi-weekly strategy meetings

**Skills / Experience**

* 0.5 - 3 years of experience
* Numerical and analytical with excellent attention to detail
* Strong systems knowledge
* Keen interest in markets
* Strong Interpersonal skills
* Ability to work well under pressure

**Salary Range**
The expected base salary for this New York, New York, United States-based position is $85000-$110000. In addition, you may be eligible for a discretionary bonus if you are an active employee as of fiscal year-end.
  
  
**Benefits**
Goldman Sachs is committed to providing our people with valuable and competitive benefits and wellness offerings, as it is a core part of providing a strong overall employee experience. A summary of these offerings, which are generally available to active, non-temporary, full-time and part-time US employees who work at least 20 hours per week, can be found here .

jobs.title: Private Credit Analyst/Associate
jobs.description:
Our client a global investment platform is seeking to add an investment professional to its Private Credit team, investing across a flexible mandate spanning unitranche, second lien, mezzanine, holdco financings, and preferred equity. The team operates as generalists, investing across industries and transaction types, with a focus on structuring-driven, risk-adjusted returns in middle-market situations.

The role provides direct exposure to the full investment process, including underwriting, execution, and ongoing portfolio management, working closely with senior investors in a lean team environment.

**Responsibilities**

* Evaluate and underwrite private credit investment opportunities across a range of structures and industries
* Lead diligence workstreams with sponsors, management teams, advisors, and internal stakeholders
* Build and analyze detailed financial models and assess key value drivers, downside scenarios, and capital structures
* Prepare investment materials and contribute to investment committee discussions
* Develop differentiated investment views through fundamental analysis and data-driven research
* Support execution and ongoing monitoring of portfolio investments

If this sounds of interest or you would like to know more please apply.

jobs.title: Jr. Marketing Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Role Overview

The Segment Marketing team is seeking a data-driven Marketing Analyst who can also flex into Marketing Traffic Coordinator responsibilities. This hybrid role is approximately 75% focused on marketing analytics and insights and 25% focused on coordinating marketing workflow and execution.

The ideal candidate has a strong analytical background, an interest in marketing strategy, and is comfortable supporting both data-driven decision-making and the operational side of marketing campaigns. Financial services experience is not required.

Key Responsibilities

Marketing Analytics (Primary Focus)

* Analyze marketing and distribution channel performance to identify trends, opportunities, and optimization recommendations.
* Support test design, execution, and post-test analysis to drive client engagement and inform multi-channel marketing strategies.
* Conduct audience segmentation and pull data from multiple sources to support targeted marketing efforts.
* Build and maintain reports, dashboards, and presentations that translate data into clear, actionable insights for Marketing Project Managers and stakeholders.
* Review and support basic forecasting models for planning and goal-setting.
* Present findings and insights to business partners to help inform marketing strategy.

Traffic Coordination (Secondary Focus)

* Coordinate the flow of marketing work from intake through completion, ensuring deadlines and process requirements are met.
* Support Segment Marketing teams by managing campaigns and navigating internal systems for reviews, approvals, and distribution.
* Perform proofing and quality control to maintain high standards for marketing deliverables.
* Track project status, manage priorities, and communicate updates through meetings and status reports.
* Collect stakeholder feedback and translate it into clear next steps and action items.

Required Qualifications (Combined)

* 1–2 years of experience in marketing analytics, business analytics, data science, marketing operations, project coordination, or a related role.
* Must have a Marketing degree/minor or experience supporting marketing teams within a firm
* Strong analytical skills with the ability to tell a story with data and translate insights into business recommendations.
* Hands-on experience with Excel and data visualization tools such as Power BI and/or Tableau.
* Ability to gather and analyze data from multiple sources; experience with basic SQL queries is a plus.
* Python experience is nice to have, but not required.
* Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with experience working cross-functionally and presenting findings to stakeholders.
* Interest in marketing strategy, campaign execution, and the creative/marketing workflow.

jobs.title: Credit Research Analyst
jobs.description:
**Position: Associate – Leveraged Finance Research (New York)**

The Leveraged Finance platform manages high yield bonds, leveraged loans, and CLOs across global markets, with investment professionals based in the Americas, Europe, and Asia.

This Associate role sits within the Leveraged Finance Research Team in New York. The ideal candidate has experience in leveraged finance and prior sector coverage as a credit research analyst, with strong financial modeling and analytical skills.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Lead industry coverage and evaluate investment opportunities across high yield and leveraged loan markets, assessing relative value across capital structures
* Underwrite and assess credit risk to support portfolio positioning decisions
* Partner with Capital Markets and external counterparties on new issue bonds and leveraged loans
* Mentor and oversee junior credit research team members
* Maintain cross-functional relationships with Portfolio Management, Product Strategy, Capital Markets, Legal, and other internal stakeholders
* Develop thematic research materials and investment insights

jobs.title: Entry Level Analyst
jobs.description:
**Our Client, a Global Financial Services Firm, is seeking an Entry Level Analyst in their Boston, MA location.**

***The Operations Specialist is responsible for ensuring the accurate and timely entry of all trade activity into the accounting system while supporting core securities processing functions across the firm’s business lines. This role plays a critical part in trade control execution, cash management, reconciliation, issue resolution, and 529 plan administration, helping to minimize risk and maintain operational integrity.***

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Ensure all trades (straight-through processed and manual) are accurately recorded in the accounting system
* Reconcile trades between order management and trade processing systems
* Research and resolve trade price deviations and discrepancies with appropriate stakeholders
* Manage daily cash flow trading activities with investment companies, including trade confirmation oversight
* Coordinate cash-raising activities for fund portfolios to support expense payments (e.g., management fees)
* Oversee portfolio reallocation events to ensure alignment with target allocations
* Provide cash projections to clients and internal stakeholders using multiple reporting tools
* Validate systemic feeds and manual adjustments to ensure accurate and timely cash reporting
* Communicate proactively with internal and external partners to ensure proper trade accountability and risk mitigation
* Partner closely with Trade Processing to maintain operational efficiency and strong team collaboration

**Qualifications & Skills:**

* BA/BS degree or equivalent work experience
* Strong PC and systems proficiency
* Ability to identify, escalate, and resolve issues effectively
* Professional verbal and written communication skills
* Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a deadline-driven environment
* Proven ability to work both independently and within a team setting

jobs.title: Junior Financial Analyst
jobs.description:
iHeartMedia Markets
  
  
Current employees and contingent workers click here
**to apply and search by the Job Posting Title.**
The audio revolution is here – and iHeart is leading it! iHeartMedia,
**the number one audio company in America**
, reaches 90% of Americans every month -- a monthly audience that’s
**twice the size of any other audio company**
– almost three times the size of the largest TV network – and almost 4 times the size of the largest ad-supported music streaming service. In fact, we have:
  
  

* More #1 rated markets than the next two largest radio companies combined;
* We’re the largest podcast publisher, with more monthly downloads than the second- and third-largest podcast publishers combined. Podcasting, the fastest-growing new media, today has more monthly users than streaming music services or Netflix;
* iHeart is the home of many of the country’s most popular and trusted on-air personalities and podcast influencers, who build important connections with hundreds of communities across America;
* We create and produce some of the most popular and well-known branded live music events in America, including the iHeartRadio Music Festival, the iHeartRadio Music Awards, the iHeartCountry Festival, iHeartRadio Fiesta Latina and the iHeartRadio Jingle Ball Tour;
* iHeartRadio is the #1 streaming radio digital service in America;
* Our social media footprint is 7 times larger than the next largest audio service; and
* We have the only complete audio ad technology stack in the industry for all forms of audio, from on demand to broadcast radio, digital streaming radio and podcasting, which bring data, targeting and attribution to all forms of audio at an unparalleled scale. As a result, we’re able to combine our strong leadership position in audience reach, usage and ad tech with powerful tools and insights for our sales organizations to help them build success for their clients at a more efficient cost than any other option.

Because we reach almost every community in America, we’re committed to providing a range of programming that reflects the diversity of the many communities we serve – and our company reflects that same kind of diversity. Our company values stress collaboration, curiosity, welcoming dissent, accepting mistakes in the pursuit of new ideas, and respect for everyone.
  
  
Only one company in America has the #1 position in everything audio: iHeartMedia!
  
  
If you’re excited about this role but don’t feel your experience aligns perfectly with the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. At iHeartMedia we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace and are looking for teammates passionate about what we do!
  
  
**What We Need:**
We are seeking a motivated and detail oriented Junior Financial Analyst to support the Multi-Platform Group finance team. This person will directly report to the Director of Finance. This role is ideal for someone early in their career who is eager to learn, build strong analytical skills, and gain exposure to financial reporting and business insights in a fast paced environment.
  
  
The Junior Financial Analyst will work closely with senior analysts, managers, and directors assisting with weekly and monthly deliverables while developing proficiency in financial systems, data analysis, and presentation of insights.
  
  
This is an in-person role in Midtown Manhattan.
  
  
**What You'll Do:**

* Maintain weekly and monthly financial reporting packages including relevant analysis and takeaways
* Contribute to analyses that support business decision making including budgeting, forecasting, and tracking KPIs
* Help perform data analysis deep dives to identify trends, drivers, and anomalies under the guidance of senior team members
* Interface with business, finance & sales leads, accounting partners, and IT to ensure accurate data and information gathering
* Assist with data reconciliation between systems to ensure consistency and reliability of financial information
* Prepare PowerPoint presentations summarizing financial results and key takeaways

**What You'll Need:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business, or a related field
* Some proficiency in Microsoft Excel (e.g., formulas, pivot tables, basic data analysis)
* 0 to 2 years of experience in finance, operations, revenue management, or related field
* Strong critical thinking and analytical skills
* Excellent communication
* Strong attention to detail
* Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
* Curious and eager to learn

**What You'll Bring:**

* Respect for others and a strong belief that others should do this in return
* Business insight and ability to apply general knowledge of business
* Individual accountability and understanding of when to seek guidance
* Skills managing assigned projects to completion using instruction, guidance, and direction
* Skills to solve problems within standardized procedures and practices
* Ability to communicate straightforward information in a digestible manner and an understanding of when to ask questions in order to validate understanding
* Understanding of impact of own decisions
* General understanding of business principles

**Compensation:**
Salary to be determined by multiple factors including but not limited to relevant experience, knowledge, skills, other job-related qualifications,
  
  
and alignment with market data.
  
  
$66,300 - $75,000
  
  
**Location:**
New York, NY: 125 West 55th Street, 10019
  
  
**Position Type:**
Regular
  
  
**Time Type:**
Full time
  
  
**Pay Type:**
Salaried
  
  
**Benefits:**
iHeartMedia’s benefits offering is flexible and offers a variety of choices to meet the diverse needs of our changing workforce, including the following:
  
  

* Employer sponsored medical, dental and vision with a variety of coverage options
* Company provided and supplemental life insurance
* Paid vacation and sick time
* Paid company holidays
* A Spirit day to encourage and allow our employees to more easily volunteer in their community
* A 401K plan
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP) at no cost – services include telephonic counseling sessions, consultation on legal and financial matters, emotional well-being, family and caregiving
* A range of additional voluntary programs, such as spending accounts, student loan refinancing, accident insurance and more!

We are accepting applications for this role on an ongoing basis.
  
  
The Company is an equal opportunity employer and will not tolerate discrimination in employment on the basis of race, color, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, religion, disability, ethnicity, national origin, marital status, protected veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected classification or status.
  
  
Non-Compete will be required for certain positions and as allowed by law.
  
  
Our organization participates in E-Verify. Click here to learn about E-Verify.

jobs.title: Analyst
jobs.description:
**Job Title:**
Analyst 1, Technology Investment Banking

**Location:**
New York, NY, in office

**Firm Overview:**

Cantor Fitzgerald L.P., with over 12,000 employees, has been a leading global financial services firm at the forefront of financial and technological innovation since 1945. Cantor Fitzgerald & Co. is a premier investment bank serving more than 5,000 institutional clients globally, recognized for its expertise in equity capital markets and fixed income, mergers and acquisitions, SPAC underwriting, and PIPEs and private placements, alongside a strong global distribution platform.

**Responsibilities:**

The Technology Investment Banking Group at Cantor Fitzgerald is seeking highly motivated, successful candidates for the Analyst 1 position in New York. The Technology Investment Banking Group offers Analysts an exceptional opportunity to work on small deal teams with direct exposure to clients and senior bankers. Analysts are provided significant responsibility within the full transaction execution process, including preparing pitch materials, creating financial analyses, preparing marketing materials, and drafting internal committee memoranda. Analysts are expected to receive exposure across various Technology verticals.

* Prepare and analyze historical and projected financial information
* Perform financial analyses and create financial models
* Coordinate industry and company-specific due diligence related to transactions
* Perform industry research to support pitches and new business generation activities using tools such as S&P Capital IQ, FactSet, and PitchBook
* Prepare confidential information memoranda, management presentations, and pitches for clients

**Requirements & Qualifications:**

* Undergraduate degree with strong academic track record
* Relevant internship experience/less than 1 year of related financial services experience
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Strong grasp of corporate finance, valuation, and accounting
* Advanced PowerPoint and Excel skills
* Demonstrated interest or experience in Technology
* Minimum cumulative GPA of 3.5/4.0
* SIE, Series 63 & Series 79 licensing required (or to be obtained upon employment)

**Salary:**
$100,000 - $125,000

* The expected base salary for this position ranges from $100,000 to $125,000 per year. The actual base salary will be determined on an individualized basis taking into account a wide range of factors including, but not limited to, relevant skills, experience, education, and, where applicable, licenses or certifications held. In addition to base salary and a competitive benefits package, this position may be eligible for additional types of compensation including discretionary bonuses and other short- and long-term incentives (e.g., deferred cash, equity, etc.).

jobs.title: Asset & Wealth Management, Fixed Income, Corporate Credit / Investment Grade Trader, Analyst, New York
jobs.description:
**Job Description**
**Asset & Wealth Management Overview**
A career with Goldman Sachs Asset & Wealth Management is an opportunity to help clients across the globe realize their potential, while you discover your own. As part of one of the world’s leading asset managers with over $3 trillion\* in assets under supervision, you can expect to participate in exciting investment opportunities while collaborating with talented colleagues from all asset classes and regions and building meaningful relationships with your clients.
  
  
**Fixed Income & Liquidity Solutions Overview**
Fixed Income and Liquidity Solutions, within Public Investing, manages more than $1.7 trillion\* in assets for a variety of clients, including private wealth mandates and institutional clients such as insurance companies, pension funds, and endowments. The team has a long history of investing across public fixed income strategies including rates, currency and credit on behalf of our clients. Investment ideas are created through collaboration between portfolio managers, research analysts, and traders.
  
  
**Role Overview**
The Global Corporate Credit team is actively recruiting a highly motivated Investment Grade Trader at the Analyst/Associate level, based in New York. This individual will play a pivotal role within our dynamic trading and portfolio management team, directly contributing to the strategic management of investment-grade credit allocation across a diverse range of portfolios and investment strategies.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Collaborate with credit portfolio managers and research analysts to identify and capitalize on value opportunities within the investment-grade corporate bond market.
* Proactively identify, analyze, and execute security selection and relative value strategies to enhance portfolio performance.
* Partner with credit and client portfolio managers to construct, monitor, and manage credit risk exposures and cash flows across portfolios.
* Cultivate and maintain robust relationships with sell-side trading desks and syndicate teams to optimize market access and execution.
* Formulate recommendations, allocate capital, and execute trades efficiently and accurately.
* Enhance and maintain proprietary trading tools, ensuring the integrity and accuracy of underlying data.

**Qualifications**

* Experience:

+ Minimum 2 years experience in fixed income trading, sales, or related analytical roles.
+ Direct trading experience preferred. Aptitude considered for Analyst level.

* Market Knowledge:

+ Understanding of global fixed income and credit markets.
+ Interest in corporate bonds and macroeconomics.

* Analytical Skills:

+ Strong quantitative and analytical skills.
+ Attention to detail for financial modeling, valuation, and risk assessment.

* Communication:

+ Strong verbal and written communication skills.
+ Ability to articulate market insights and strategies.
+ Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders.

* Work Ethic:

+ Proactive, results-oriented, and accountable work ethic.
+ Initiative and resilience in a fast-paced trading environment.

* Industry Background:

+ Asset management experience is an advantage, not required.

**Salary Range**
The expected base salary for this New York, New York, United States-based position is $85000-$110000. In addition, you may be eligible for a discretionary bonus if you are an active employee as of fiscal year-end.
  
  
**Benefits**
Goldman Sachs is committed to providing our people with valuable and competitive benefits and wellness offerings, as it is a core part of providing a strong overall employee experience. A summary of these offerings, which are generally available to active, non-temporary, full-time and part-time US employees who work at least 20 hours per week, can be found here .

jobs.title: Financial Analyst
jobs.description:
**Title:**
Financial Analyst

**Pay:**
$28.00- $36.00/hour

**Location:**
Juno Beach, FL (On-Site)

**Schedule:**
Standard Office Hours

**Duration:**
12-Month Contract

Role Overview

The position supports senior management decision-making through financial modeling and analysis. This includes revenue requirement modeling, customer bill impact analysis, discounted cash flow (DCF), and financial statement modeling.

The role involves conducting moderately complex financial analyses to evaluate business decisions and recommending strategies to enhance financial performance.

Key Responsibilities

* Analyze and communicate the financial impact of business decisions on company structure, profitability, and competitiveness using appropriate analytical methods and forecasting models
* Support financial evaluation of projects, including revenue requirements, net present value (NPV), and customer bill impacts
* Provide financial expertise, guidance, and training to internal stakeholders
* Perform financial analyses required by management or regulatory authorities
* Develop and deliver presentations for senior and executive leadership
* Carry out additional duties as assigned

Required Qualifications

* High School Diploma or GED
* Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience
* Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience

Desired Qualifications

* Understanding of fundamental accounting principles
* Ability to interpret and analyze financial statements
* Experience with SAP reporting tools (e.g., BusinessObjects)
* Familiarity with utility industry systems and processes

Preferred Qualifications

* Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance
* Master's degree in Business Administration (MBA)

jobs.title: Sr. Financial Analyst
jobs.description:
Newmark Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: NMRK), together with its subsidiaries (“Newmark”), is a world leader in commercial real estate, seamlessly powering every phase of the property life cycle. Newmark’s comprehensive suite of services and products is uniquely tailored to each client, from owners to occupiers, investors to founders, and startups to blue-chip companies. Combining the platform’s global reach with market intelligence in both established and emerging property markets, Newmark provides superior service to clients across the industry spectrum. For the twelve months ended September 30, 2025, Newmark generated revenues of over $3.1 billion. As of September 30, 2025, Newmark and its business partners together operated from approximately 170 offices with over 8,500 professionals across four continents. To learn more, visit nmrk.com or follow @newmark.

**Job Summary:**

Primary role is to underwrite apartment communities. Additionally, provides financial analyses, analytical decision support services, and technical expertise for a broad array of financial and operational projects and initiatives. Design and build sophisticated financial models based on diverse data sets, in order to facilitate strategic decision making.

**Essential Job Duties:**

* Financial modeling and underwriting prospective assignments; individual loans or portfolio of loans as well as investment properties to include but not limited to multi-family and land.
* Assist with due diligence as required for underwriting and marketing to include appraisals, property condition reports, environmental reports, income and expense statements, rent rolls, mortgage, note, modifications, forbearance agreements, foreclosure filings, and title searches, etc.
* Assists in obtaining revenue and assets acquisition/ disposition information. May review, consolidate, and summarize information for valuation and marketing purposes.
* May coordinate financial analysis matters with other departments, locations and divisions.
* Develop/assist in design of offering and presentation packages.
* Research for comparable sold and for sale listings, and submarket statistics such as competitive inventory, availability, lease rates, etc.
* Assist with public records research for potential assignment opportunities.
* Research local economic drivers and write market overviews.

**Qualifications:**

* Must be detail oriented
* Ability to comprehend, analyze, and interpret complex financial information and transactions and accounting principles.
* Ability to problem-solve, both independently and working as a team. Requires strong analytical and quantitative skills.
* Manage time effectively and work on several projects simultaneously
* Excellent written and oral communication skills
* Ability to provide efficient, timely, reliable and courteous service to clients. Ability to effectively present information in written and verbal form to teammates, clients and supervisors.

**Skills, Education and Experience:**

* Bachelor’s degree (Real Estate, Economics, Finance, Mathematics, or Statistics majors preferred)
* Minimum 2+ years of commercial real estate, private equity, or investment banking experience with an emphasis in financial modeling
* Successful applicants should be highly motivated and highly analytical with a passion for commercial real estate, loan and investment sales, and finance
* Advanced knowledge of Excel
* High-level understanding of financial concepts such as IRR, NPV, Cash on Cash
* Demonstrated research ability
* Knowledge of CoStar, Yardi Matrix, and other real estate data sources is a plus
* Knowledge of loan documents and terminology
* May perform other duties as assigned

**Working Conditions:**
Normal working conditions with the absence of disagreeable elements.

**Note**
: The statements herein are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees, and are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of responsibilities, duties, and skills required of personnel so classified.

* Newmark is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex including sexual orientation and gender identity, national origin, disability, protected Veteran Status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

jobs.title: Research Analyst, Private Markets (EquityZen)
jobs.description:
Morgan Stanley is a leading global financial services firm providing a wide range of investment banking, securities, investment management and wealth management services. The Firm's employees serve clients worldwide including corporations, governments and individuals from more than 1,200 offices in 43 countries.
  
  
As a market leader, the talent and passion of our people is critical to our success. Together, we share a common set of values rooted in integrity, excellence and strong team ethic. Morgan Stanley can provide a superior foundation for building a professional career - a place for people to learn, to achieve and grow. A philosophy that balances personal lifestyles, perspectives and needs is an important part of our culture.
  
  
EquityZen, a part of Morgan Stanley, is a leading marketplace for pre-IPO investments, connecting shareholders of private companies with accredited investors. Through its category-leading technology and innovative approach, EquityZen delivers data-driven insights and tools that empower its 800,000 users to navigate the private markets with confidence. Having completed more than 51,000 private placements in nearly 500 private companies, EquityZen is on a mission to deliver “Private Markets for the Public.” EquityZen is looking for a detail oriented and analytical
**Research Analyst**
to join our growing team.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Build capitalization (“cap”) tables for private companies using information found in public filings. These cap tables are a key component of the investment due diligence for investors in private companies
* Assist with with the construction of offering documents for new private company investment offerings
* Complete pricing / valuation analysis for private companies
* Provide research for individual pre-IPO companies
* Work collaboratively with other teams (Sales, Operations, Compliance, Marketing, and Product) on a wide range of projects/initiatives
* Keep up with pre-IPO news, public filings and market trends and use insights to drive new research initiatives

**Qualifications**

* Undergraduate degree required
* 1-2 + years of professional experience in financial services, technology, or venture capital
* Fundamental investment research or analysis, preferred
* Demonstrated excellence in writing and communication skills; preferably for a range of audiences (technical and non-technical) to present concise content
* Qualified candidates may be asked to provide a writing sample during interview process
* Basic understanding of corporate finance and accounting concepts
* Desire to learn about tech companies across markets and industry verticals
* Strong critical thinking skills. Candidates should be able to demonstrate their ability to think through investment implications of industry news/events
* Must be very detail-oriented, with an ability to multi-task and handle multiple priorities
* Desire to thrive in a collaborative environment, leveraging strong interpersonal skills
* Ability to present findings, implications and recommendations to colleagues and senior stakeholders
* Intermediate technical skills; including advanced Word, Excel and PowerPoint skills
* Familiarity with Pitchbook and Crunchbase is a plus

**What You Can Expect From Morgan Stanley**
At Morgan Stanley, we raise, manage and allocate capital for our clients – helping them reach their goals. We do it in a way that’s differentiated – and we’ve done that for 90 years. Our values - putting clients first, doing the right thing, leading with exceptional ideas, committing to diversity and inclusion, and giving back - aren’t just beliefs, they guide the decisions we make every day to do what's best for our clients, communities and more than 80,000 employees in 1,200 offices across 42 countries. At Morgan Stanley, you’ll find an opportunity to work alongside the best and the brightest, in an environment where you are supported and empowered. Our teams are relentless collaborators and creative thinkers, fueled by their diverse backgrounds and experiences. We are proud to support our employees and their families at every point along their work-life journey, offering some of the most attractive and comprehensive employee benefits and perks in the industry. There’s also ample opportunity to move about the business for those who show passion and grit in their work.
  
  
To learn more about our offices across the globe, please copy and paste https://www.morganstanley.com/about-us/global-offices into your browser.
  
  
Expected base pay rates for the role will be between $57,000 and $115,000 per year at the commencement of employment. However, base pay if hired will be determined on an individualized basis and is only part of the total compensation package, which, depending on the position, may also include commission earnings, incentive compensation, discretionary bonuses, other short and long-term incentive packages, and other Morgan Stanley sponsored benefit programs.
  
  
Morgan Stanley's goal is to build and maintain a workforce that is diverse in experience and background but uniform in reflecting our standards of integrity and excellence. Consequently, our recruiting efforts reflect our desire to attract and retain the best and brightest from all talent pools. We want to be the first choice for prospective employees.
  
  
It is the policy of the Firm to ensure equal employment opportunity without discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, religion, creed, age, sex, sex stereotype, gender, gender identity or expression, transgender, sexual orientation, national origin, citizenship, disability, marital and civil partnership/union status, pregnancy, veteran or military service status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law.
  
  
Morgan Stanley is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversifying its workforce (M/F/Disability/Vet).

jobs.title: Strategy Analyst
jobs.description:
The Strategy team at Heidrick and Struggles works closely with the executive leadership team (CEO, President, CFO, Chief Legal Officer and CHROROCHCH) and business line leadership to identify and address the company’s most impactful opportunities and challenges to improve Heidrick’s short- and long-term business performance. The Analyst will be a key part of the Strategy team providing critical analytical, research, and logistical support to the Vice President and Senior Manager of Strategy. This role works on the company’s highest-priority initiatives, including annual strategic planning, competitive analysis, M&A evaluations, and business performance deep-dives. The Analyst will be responsible for building complex financial models, conducting market research, and creating compelling presentations for the executive leadership team. This position offers a unique opportunity to gain exposure to the firm's global operations and influence its long-term direction.

**Responsibilities:**

* Quantitative and Financial Analysis: develop and maintain sophisticated financial models to support strategic decision-making and measure the impact of initiatives
* Strategic Research and Due Diligence: conduct and synthesize in-depth research on industry trends, competitor activities, and potential strategic partners or M&A targets
* Presentation and Communication Support: translate complex analytical findings into visually compelling slides and narratives for executive-level presentations. Assist in the preparation and facilitation of the annual strategic planning process and workshops
* Project and Initiative Support: provide organizational and logistical support for high-priority strategic projects. Track and report on the progress of ongoing strategic initiatives against defined metrics and timelines



**Required Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance or related field
* Minimum two-three years’ experience in a similar, analytically rigorous role in a corporation, leading strategy consulting firm or financial services institution (e.g., investment bank or private equity firm)
* Expert-level proficiency in statistical and financial modeling in Excel – proven ability to create statistical models to identify key trends and patterns in external and internal data, forecast business performance, simulate the impact of potential strategic options and decisions
* Experience creating executive level presentations in PowerPoint

* Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment and to prioritize multiple competing responsibilities
* Demonstrated ability for independent thinking and creative problem-solving while collaborating effectively as a team player
* Proven ability to communicate clearly and effectively, both verbally and in writing, with diverse audiences at all levels of the organization

**Preferred Qualifications:**

* Familiarity with data visualization tools like Power BI
* Experience working in a global, multi product or service line organization
* Experience in a professional services organization

jobs.title: Senior Financial Analyst I ( Sales)
jobs.description:
**Staples is business to business. You’re what binds us together.**

Our talented finance team partners with every area of the business to drive results and provide financial expertise. We are passionate about analyzing and interpreting information to assess performance and provide guidance. Our team serves as a true partner and advisor to all our business leaders; working with them to accelerate the company’s profitability and growth.

As a
**Senior Financial Analyst,**
you will play a key role in analyzing sales and margin performance, customer trends, and profitability across our diverse client base. Your insights will guide strategic decisions, support sales leadership, and help strengthen customer relationships through data driven storytelling and financial expertise. This role partners with Sales, Operations, and Finance, and plays a meaningful part in shaping revenue and margin outcomes.

**What you’ll be doing:**

* Own recurring weekly and monthly reporting on sales, margin, and financial performance.
* Support annual budgeting and recurring forecast cycles across verticals, categories, and sales coverage models.
* Partner closely with Sales Operations to analyze pipeline activity, win/ramp trends, and conversion forecasts.
* Build and maintain financial models that support strategic initiatives and provide data-driven recommendations to leadership.
* Prepare clear, executive-ready materials for monthly business reviews and leadership discussions.
* Translate financial results and performance trends into actionable insights for cross-functional partners.
* Leverage advanced Excel and data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar) to strengthen reporting, automate processes, and scale analytics.

**What’s needed – Basic Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in finance, accounting, economics, business administration, or a related field. Or equivalent work experience.
* Minimum of 3-5 years of experience in financial analysis, forecasting, or budgeting roles.
* Demonstrated proficiency in advanced Excel and financial modeling.
* Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities effectively

**What’s needed – Preferred Qualifications:**

* Master’s degree in finance, economics, or MBA.
* Experience in a sales or revenue-focused environment.
* Familiarity with analytics and BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Alteryx, or Snowflake
* Strong business acumen and ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders
* Experience supporting strategic initiatives and developing business cases through financial modeling

**We Offer
:**

* Inclusive culture with associate-led Business Resource Groups
* Flexible PTO (22 days) and Holiday Schedule (7 observed paid holidays)
* Online and Retail Discounts, Company Match 401(k), Physical and Mental Health Wellness programs, and more perks and benefits

jobs.title: Hedge Fund - Credit Analyst
jobs.description:
We are currently working with an exciting credit fund that are looking to expand their corporate credit team. The PM is looking for an analyst to complement the team, this is an excellent opportunity for an entrepreneurial individual to continue learning and developing their skills further.

Responsibilities:

* Conduct detailed fundamental credit research across US HY corporates.
* Provide well formulated long/short investment recommendation to the portfolio manager.
* Build and maintain your own financial models.
* Keep informed on wider market trends, such as macroeconomic developments, market sentiment, and industry condition.

Candidate Profile:

* c.3+ years of experience covering US HY corporate credit.
* Buy side experience is preferred however open to candidates coming from a credit research desk at a leading investment bank.
* Excellent modelling skills and the ability to incorporate wider macro themes into your investment recommendations.

jobs.title: Financial Analyst - Middle Office  (MAx $51/hr W2)
jobs.description:
**Company Description**
Finance
  
  
**Job Description**
We are looking for an Operations Analyst to support daily reconciliations and data analysis within a Middle Office environment. This role is well-suited for someone with an operations background who is comfortable executing repeatable processes, working with data, and supporting senior team members.

* Exposure to asset management, banking operations, or financial services environments

jobs.title: Investment Banking Analyst | Mergers & Acquisitions
jobs.description:
Jefferies’ M&A team in New York is currently seeking Analysts to support mergers & acquisitions and strategic advisory assignments across multiple industries. The role offers significant responsibility in both pitching and live deal execution.

**Primary responsibilities:**

* Assist in the execution of M&A and advisory transactions.
* Prepare and deliver client presentations for pitches and live deals.
* Build and utilize detailed financial models, including discounted cash flow, leveraged buyout, and multiples-based analyses
* Draft materials including offering memoranda, confidential information memoranda, management and lender presentations, and prospectuses.
* Conduct industry research, analyze business plans, and review detailed financial information.
* Participate in due diligence processes and drafting sessions.
* Prepare internal deal memos and coordinate across senior and junior deal team members.
* Manage multiple projects simultaneously in a fast‑paced environment.

**Required background and qualifications:**

* At least one year of M&A related experience (direct investment banking experience, consulting or valuations)
* Strong technical skills, including financial modeling and valuation.
* Strong written and verbal communication ability.
* Ability to manage multiple transactions simultaneously; high attention to detail and strong work ethic.
* Self‑starter who can operate independently as well as within deal teams.
* Bachelor’s degree with strong academic performance.

jobs.title: Financial Analyst
jobs.description:
**About Us:**

BBVA Global Wealth Advisors, part of the BBVA Group, is an SEC-registered investment adviser committed to providing exceptional financial advice and investment management services to our clients. We are dedicated to upholding the highest fiduciary standards and ensuring the best interests of our clients is always at the forefront of our operations.

**Position Summary:**

We are seeking a motivated, detail-oriented and proactive Financial Analyst to join our Finance department. This role is ideal for an individual with 3-5 years of experience who is interested in a career in financial services. Professional working proficiency in both Spanish and English is a mandatory requirement for this role to effectively serve our diverse stakeholders.

**Areas of responsibilities include:**

**Reporting**

* Prepare detailed reports and presentations for internal stakeholders to drive operational efficiency and support strategic decision-making.
* Communicate financial insights and findings clearly and effectively in both Spanish and English.
* Perform ad-hoc analysis and support various finance projects as needed.

**General Accounting and Month-End Process**

* Perform in-depth analysis of each P&L line, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow statement to ensure accurate financial structure and reporting
* Conduct thorough review and analysis of daily/weekly financial reports and monthly accounting reconciliations
* Coordinate back-end activities for month-end closing, ensuring all accounting procedures are updated and financial data is accurate for the reporting period
* Execute monthly financial reporting to the headquarters in Madrid
* Proactively monitor and perform deep budget analysis (actuals vs budget) to report on performance deviations
* Participate in the yearly budgeting process providing analytical input following

**Procurement and Internal Governance**

* Lead analysis and improvement initiatives for core finance processes and workflows.
* Support the procurement process end to end by analyzing company needs, vendor proposals and collaborating with the team to formalize contracts
* Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal policies.

**Required qualifications**

* Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field.
* **3-5 years of experience**
  in a financial analysis or accounting role,
  **with demonstrated experience in process definition, improvement, and internal data analysis.**
* Understanding of procurement processes and basic knowledge of Third Party Risk Management (TPRM).
* Complete professional fluency in both Spanish and English (written and verbal).
* Strong analytical and quantitative skills with
  **excellent attention to detail.**
* Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (e.g., pivot tables, VLOOKUP, financial functions).
* The "
  **Lean" Mindset:**
  Ability to work autonomously in a 3-person team, taking full "end-to-end" ownership of projects.

**Preferred experience**

* Experience working in a Registered Investment Advisor (RIA), Broker dealer or other financial services firm, so Series 65 is highly valued but not required
* Familiarity with financial and enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems (e.g., Workday, Concur, Bill.com, Sage Intacct, Aravo).

jobs.title: Economic Damages & Valuations Internship - Summer 2026
jobs.description:
Are you looking to join an organization that is growing and dynamic? What about a high-energy, collaborative environment that rewards hard work?

J.S. Held is a global consulting firm that combines technical, scientific, financial, and strategic expertise to advise clients seeking to realize value and mitigate risk. Our professionals serve as trusted advisors to organizations facing high stakes matters demanding urgent attention, staunch integrity, proven experience, clear-cut analysis, and an understanding of both tangible and intangible assets.

The firm provides a comprehensive suite of services, products, and data that enable clients to navigate complex, contentious, and often catastrophic situations.

Whether it is honing your skills or building your network, we know that success cannot come without growth. Our programs equip you with the knowledge and training you need to play a valuable role on your team and establish a long-term career.

**Job Description**

As an Intern on the Economic Damages & Valuations Team, your responsibilities will extend beyond those of traditional interns. The ideal person for this role will need to not only have a strong grasp of accounting and finance concepts, but also be flexible and a strategic thinker able to take on a variety of tasks.

In this role, you will support a wide range of litigation-focused engagements, including economic damages assessments, business valuation analyses, and forensic accounting. Typical assignments may involve commercial damages, business interruption claims, fraud investigations, partnership and trust disputes, family law matters, shareholder disputes, and other specialized projects.

Job Responsibilities:

* Perform comprehensive analysis of financial statements, tax returns, and transactional data to develop financial models and support data‑driven decision‑making.
* Conduct in‑depth company, industry, and product research.
* Input financial statement, tax return, and transactional data.
* Assist with drafting expert reports.
* Prepare data for demonstrative tables, charts, and graphs.
* Organize documents and maintain orderly records.
* Perform quality‑control checks.
* Attend internal training events.

**Qualifications**

**Required Qualifications**

* Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance with a minimum GPA of 3.0 in both your major and overall.
* Currently working towards the successful completion of accounting and finance courses prior to the start of your internship.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
* Interest in the Finance, Forensic Accounting, Business Valuation, or related fields.
* Exhibit excellent communication skills and the ability to work in a team environment.

**Physical and Mental Job Qualifications**

* Must be willing to work in person in our San Francisco, CA office on a hybrid schedule.

**Additional Information**

Some of the Benefits We Have Include

J.S. Held understands all our employees are people and sometimes life needs flexibility. We work to always provide an environment that best supports and suits our team’s needs.

* Our flexible work environment allows employees to work remotely, when needed

A reasonable estimate of the salary range for this role is $20 - $28 per hour. Any offered salary range is based on a wide array of factors including but not limited to skillset, experience, training, location, scope of role, management responsibility, etc.

Other Duties

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

By submitting your application, you acknowledge that you have read the J.S. Held Online Privacy Notice and hereby freely and unambiguously give informed consent to the collection, processing, use, and storage of your personal information as required and described therein. California residents can click here to learn more about the personal information we collect and here to learn about additional privacy rights that may be available.

Please explore what we’re all about at www.jsheld.com.

EEO and Job Accommodations

*We embrace diversity and our commitment to building a team and environment that fosters professional and personal enrichment is unwavering. We are greater when we are equal!*

*J.S. Held is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to hiring a diverse workforce. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected Veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.*

jobs.title: Business Management , Private Equity Solutions Group, Business Analyst - New York

jobs.title: Financial Analyst
jobs.description:
**Overview:**
The Financial Analyst reporting directly to the Associate Finance Director of Revvity Omics and Viacord is a visible and challenging position working with all levels within the company to deliver timely and accurate reporting and analysis of Revvity’s financial performance. The position requires a highly motivated self-starter who is extremely analytical and has excellent interpersonal skills to collaborate with multiple teams within the enterprise. This is a hybrid opportunity based at our corporate headquarters in Waltham, MA.
  
  
**Role & Responsibilities:**

* Provide analytical support to the company’s Revvity Omics organization
* Identify opportunities to automate and improve reporting capabilities and assist in solutions implementation
* Support ad hoc analysis as required, including requests from the DX VP of Finance
* Own budget process and long-term modelling for Diagnostics Omics business unit
* Own monthly/quarterly forecasting and reporting processes for Diagnostics Omics business unit
* Analyze financial results and variances, provide insightful commentary, and generate content for monthly and quarterly Corporate deliverables and business reviews
* Partner with leadership teams to understand operational drivers, ensure execution of financial commitments; identify and communicate risks, opportunities, and actions for mitigation
* Work with leadership to develop and prepare clear, actionable reporting for business teams; enhance forecasting processes through increased visibility into financial metrics and performance
* Operate as main point of contact for ad-hoc and project-based support as needed for business and finance requests

**Basic Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree with 1 years’ experience in accounting or finance at multi-national, matrixed organization or 2 years in accounting or finance

**Preferred Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Finance or related field
* 3 years of progressive experience in an FP&A role
* 1+ years of experience developing dynamic financial and operational models

The base salary range for this full-time position is $64,500.00 - $85,000.00. This range reflects the minimum and maximum target for a new hire in this position. The base pay actually offered to the successful candidate will take into account internal equity, work location, and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process.
  
  
Please note that base pay is only one part of our total compensation package and is determined within a range. This range allows for the successful candidate to have an opportunity to progress within the position and develop at our company. This base pay range does not take into account bonuses, equity, or other benefits which may be applicable and are dependent on the level and position offered.
  
  
**What do we offer?**
We provide competitive and comprehensive benefits to our employees. Below are some highlights of our benefits:
  
  

* Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance Options
* Life and Disability Insurance
* Paid Time-Off
* Parental Benefits
* Compassionate Care Leave
* 401k with Company Match
* Employee Stock Purchase Plan

Learn more about Revvity’s benefits by visiting our Bswift page. Log-In instructions are provided towards the bottom of the Bswift page.
  
  
*For benefit-eligible roles only. Part-time and temporary roles may not be eligible for all benefits listed. Please reach out to your recruiter for more information.*

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
**Hiring for a Junior Data Analyst to sit onsite 3x/week in Dearborn, MI!**

**Location: Onsite 3 days per week – Dearborn MI**

**Duration:**
6-month contract to hire

**Job Description**

The JR Analyst develops and supports analytic deliverables across the business, including sales and performance reporting, marketing and campaign analytics, dealer and customer insights, and dashboard/data visualization. The role also conducts strategic ad hoc analysis to support business strategy, daily decision‑making, and understanding of customer and marketing behavior trends. Works closely with business partners to identify analytic needs and deliver actionable insights.

**Requirements / Qualifications**

* 2-4 years as a data analyst (Jr)
* BA/BS or advanced degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science, Econometrics, Computer Science, Analytics, Finance, or a related field
* Strong data‑wrangling skills, including querying, joining, manipulating, and automating datasets
* Proficiency in SQL, BigQuery, Alteryx, Power BI, Tableau, or Qlik, as well as Microsoft Office tools
* Experience analyzing business and marketing performance, including response rates, sales, incremental lift, profitability, and customer profiling
* Experience performing behavioral and cohort analysis related to engagement, retention, and conversion trends
* Expertise in sales, business, financial, customer, CX, and program analytics
* Ability to build, maintain, and support Business, Sales, and Program Performance Reporting (BPR)
* Experience presenting insights to stakeholders and creating clear, visual PowerPoint presentations
* Strong communication, collaboration, and attention to detail
* Highly self‑motivated, independent, and ethical work style

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Familiarity with automotive, vehicle sales, parts, subscription, or loyalty program data
* Experience applying AI or advanced analytics tools to business processes

**Compensation**
:

$32/hr to $35/hr.

Exact compensation may vary based on several factors, including skills, experience, and education.

Benefit packages for this role will start on the 31st day of employment and include medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as HSA, FSA, and DCFSA account options, and 401k retirement account access with employer matching. Employees in this role are also entitled to paid sick leave and/or other paid time off as provided by applicable law.

jobs.title: Jr. Data Analyst
jobs.description:
We are looking for an entry-level Data Analyst to join our team in Jersey City, New Jersey. In this role, you will contribute to optimizing data processes and support data-driven decision-making within the organization. You will work with diverse data sets, automate workflows, and design advanced data models to improve operational efficiency and analytics capabilities.
  
  
Responsibilities:
  
• Collect, transform, and manage large data sets to streamline sourcing across a wide portfolio of vendors, suppliers, and products.
  
• Standardize and cleanse data from multiple systems to ensure consistency and accuracy.
  
• Validate and aggregate incoming raw data for integration into databases and analytical models.
  
• Address data gaps across various domains to enhance overall data quality.
  
• Extract data from multiple sources to support larger analytics projects and initiatives.
  
• Promote best practices for data usage and management across the organization, including newly acquired entities.
  
• Assist in integrating analytics models into production workflows to optimize functionality.
  
• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including advanced functions such as lookups, formatting, and pivot tables.
  
• Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to apply logic to complex challenges.
  
• Demonstrated ability to quickly learn and adapt to new technical applications.
  
• Basic understanding of data analytics and management principles.
  
• Experience with SQL for data coordination and management.
  
• Familiarity with Power BI or Power Query is a plus.
  
• Effective communication and organizational skills to manage data-related projects successfully.
  
Technology Doesn't Change the World, People Do.
®

jobs.title: Business Analyst - Logistics
jobs.description:
**Business Analyst – Logistics**
**Reports To: Director of Operational Business Analytics**

**Financial Analyst Roles & Responsibilities**

* Creating and maintaining daily, weekly and monthly performance of 3rdparty and fleet operations.

jobs.title: Investment Banking Analyst - Financial Institutions
jobs.description:
**Who We Are**
Oppenheimer & Co. Inc. (“Oppenheimer”) is a leading middle-market investment bank and full-service broker-dealer. With roots tracing back to 1881, the Company is engaged in a broad range of activities in the financial services industry, including retail securities brokerage, institutional sales and trading, investment banking (both corporate and public finance), equity & fixed income research, market making, trust services and investment advisory and asset management services.
  
  
Our Investment Banking Team is committed to providing in-depth industry knowledge and delivering innovative, market-based strategies to clients in all major industries, with a specific focus on Technology, Media and Communications, Consumer and Retail, Financial Institutions, Healthcare, Industrial Growth and Services, Energy, Rental Services as well as Transportation and Logistics. Oppenheimer advises clients across strategic and financial matters, including capital raising, capital structure, mergers and acquisitions, restructuring and special situation advisory, in partnership with our product partners. This combination of industry and product groups enables the firm to deliver advice, strategies and capital with a universal banking approach to our targeted client – high growth, entrepreneurial, middle market companies.
  
  
**Job Description**
Our Financial Institutions team is actively looking for an experienced Analyst for our New York office. The candidate will be working closely with senior members of the Financial Institutions Group with opportunities to gain exposure to both sell side and buy side advisory as well as underwriting and placing equity and debt offerings for public and privately held companies.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Preparing client presentations, drafting offering memoranda, preparing internal committee memoranda, preparing management and board presentations and other materials for senior bankers as required
* Transaction execution involving direct client interaction and communication
* Managing client due diligence processes
* Conducting extensive financial modeling and valuation
* Assessing industry research and trends

**Qualifications**

* At least 2 years of experience in investment banking, primarily Financial Institutions related
* Strong Microsoft Excel skills essential
* Strong verbal and written communication skills
* Elevated attention to detail and consistency in work product
* Strong work ethic and team player
* Organized and proactive
* Dealogic, CapIQ, Bloomberg and MS Office suite knowledge strongly preferred
* Series 63, 79 FINRA licenses and Securities Industry Essentials (SIE) Exam required at time of hiring or shortly thereafter

**Compensation**
For job postings in New York City, Oppenheimer is required by law to include a reasonable estimate of the salary range for this role. This salary range is specific to New York and takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making salary decisions including but not limited to your skills, qualifications, experience, licensure and certifications, and other business and organizational needs. A reasonable estimate of the current base salary range is $100,000.00 - $110,000.00 at the time of this posting. Also, certain positions are eligible for additional forms of compensation such as discretionary bonus.
